key_insight: A multi-rank microbiome feature model identified an 8-taxa gut signature that could predict anti-PD-1 response in hepatocellular carcinoma before treatment began.
clinical_relevance: If validated externally, this approach could help stratify HCC patients earlier for immunotherapy planning using baseline stool microbiome data.
method: Reanalyzed 16S rRNA data from 35 HCC patients across 155 longitudinal samples with strict subject-level train/test splits.
Used multi-rank taxonomic feature engineering plus log-transformation to retain informative low-abundance taxa.
Compressed the neural network into a 2,916-row lookup table with frozen CLR normalization and interpolation for standalone prediction.
evidence_signal: The 8-taxa neural network achieved AUROC 0.784 ± 0.024 on held-out data and still separated responders from non-responders at baseline (AUROC 0.681 ± 0.056).
watch_item: The baseline signal is promising but small and needs prospective external validation before clinical use, especially given the limited cohort size.
paper_title: Early Prediction of Anti-PD-1 Response in Hepatocellular Carcinoma via Multi-Rank Taxonomic Feature Engineering and a LUT-Based Prediction Vehicle
